tasks.title: Workspace and Docker boot scaffold
tasks.goal: Create the Bun workspace, Node 24-compatible TypeScript config, Docker compose, Postgres 18 + pgvector service, API health stub, and demo app shell.
tasks.why: Every later task needs a reproducible local environment and one-command boot.
acceptance: Bun workspace has apps/api, apps/demo, packages/core, packages/sdk, and packages/mcp.
Node 24 runtime compatibility is documented and enforced in package metadata or CI.
docker compose starts Postgres 18 + pgvector and the API health endpoint.
All ports bind to 127.0.0.1 by default.
scripts/loop/verify.sh no longer exits with the harness-only scaffold failure.

tasks.title: Schema, migrations, seed, and synthetic tripwire
tasks.goal: Implement Drizzle schema, generated SQL migrations, synthetic seed data, precomputed embedding fixture slots, and the synthetic-only scanner.
tasks.why: The demo must prove credible clinical primitives without allowing real PHI into the repo.
acceptance: Minimum tables from the MVP plan exist with practice_id on clinical rows.
Generated SQL migrations are committed.
Seed is idempotent and writes a seed_complete marker.
Synthetic-only scanner fails on real-looking PHI patterns.
No seed email uses a non-example domain.

tasks.title: ABAC gate and hash-chained audit
tasks.goal: Build the single v0 policy gate, structured access denial, append-only audit table, and hash-chain verifier.
tasks.why: The policy denial and audit ledger are the credibility moment for health-infra builders.
acceptance: Allowed Clinician can read only Patients in their Practice, roster, and active consent scope.
Wrong Clinician receives BonfireAccessDenied with policy receipt.
Allow and deny paths append audit events.
audit_events UPDATE and DELETE fail.
Hash-chain verifier detects tampering.

tasks.title: SDK and HTTP contract
tasks.goal: Expose the locked SDK methods and matching Fastify routes from Zod schemas.
tasks.why: The SDK is the product surface for indie developers and the seam for MCP tools.
acceptance: SDK exposes every method listed in the MVP plan.
HTTP routes mirror SDK contracts.
Zod schemas are the source for request and response validation.
Fastify schemas are in-repo generated/trusted schemas only.
Invalid client inputs return structured 4xx errors.

tasks.title: Cited semantic search
tasks.goal: Implement pgvector-backed semanticSearch with precomputed vectors, citations, freshness, excludedByPolicy, and deterministic summaries.
tasks.why: Cited retrieval is the core product proof; no LLM should be needed for the v0 wow moment.
acceptance: Golden query returns the expected cited Patient/Note result.
Results include citations, freshness, excludedByPolicy, policyReceipt, auditEventId, and timings.
Policy filtering happens before output is returned.
No keyword fallback is silently used when vectors fail.
Exact vector baseline is used for the small demo corpus.

tasks.title: Demo UI search beat
tasks.goal: Build the first browser demo beat: boot-ready screen, actor context, search query, cited result, and trust receipt.
tasks.why: The artifact must be inspectable in a browser, not just via tests and SDK snippets.
acceptance: Browser shows API ready state.
User can run the scripted search query.
Result shows summary, citations, freshness, excludedByPolicy, and audit id.
Playwright covers the flow as a user would run it.
Desktop and mobile screenshots are non-overlapping and readable.

tasks.title: Denial and audit UI beat
tasks.goal: Add actor switch, denial panel, policy-as-data view, and audit ledger rail.
tasks.why: The demo must make governance visible and falsifiable.
acceptance: Switching to the wrong Clinician denies the same query.
UI displays the policy receipt on allow and deny paths.
Audit rail appends a chained denied event.
Playwright covers actor switch and denial.
No patient-identifiable details appear in logs.

tasks.title: Propose-only note flow
tasks.goal: Implement transcript-to-draft proposeNote, agent approve denial, and Clinician approveNote commit.
tasks.why: This proves agent-safe clinical writes without autonomous write access.
acceptance: proposeNote returns a deterministic draft from the synthetic transcript.
Agent actor cannot approve and the denial is audited.
Clinician actor can approve.
Approved Note becomes stored and searchable.
UI covers transcript, draft, denial, and approval.

tasks.title: FHIR export/import round-trip
tasks.goal: Export approved notes as FHIR R4 document Bundles and import them back with reference integrity.
tasks.why: FHIR in/out proves no lock-in without pretending to be a full FHIR server.
acceptance: Exported Bundle has type=document.
Composition is the first Bundle entry.
All references resolve inside the Bundle.
Import is idempotent for the demo path.
Imported Note is searchable and cited.

tasks.title: Local MCP tools
tasks.goal: Expose local-only MCP tools derived from typed SDK functions with a strict allowlist and no direct writes.
tasks.why: Agent builders need an MCP proof, but unsafe tool surfaces would destroy trust.
acceptance: Only approved tools from the MVP plan are exposed.
No approveNote, raw SQL, shell, file, or arbitrary tool is exposed.
Tool schemas derive from trusted Zod schemas.
Tool metadata is snapshot-tested.
Prompt-injection/tool-poisoning fixtures cannot alter the allowlist.

tasks.title: Docs, demo GIF, and hosted read-only playground
tasks.goal: Polish README, SECURITY, CONTRIBUTING, architecture docs, demo GIF, and optional hosted read-only playground.
tasks.why: OSS credibility depends on the repo explaining what is real, what is fake, and how to contribute.
acceptance: README quickstart works from a clean clone.
README includes GIF, SDK snippet, architecture, and what-is-real table.
SECURITY states local synthetic demo boundary.
CONTRIBUTING states no real PHI policy.
Hosted playground, if shipped, is read-only and blocks uploads/writes.
